extraArgs.description: Extra CLI args appended to the controller container.
extraArgs.title: extraArgs

extraEnv.description: Extra environment variables for the controller container.
extraEnv.title: extraEnv

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volume mounts on the controller container (pairs with extraVolumes).
extraVolumeMounts.title: extraVolumeMounts

extraVolumes.description:
Extra volumes on the controller pod. Use this to make a filesystem-backend
repository reachable in-process (hostPath/NFS/PVC) so the controller can run
short idempotent kopia ops (ADR §5.4). The e2e harness uses a hostPath here.
extraVolumes.title: extraVolumes

leaderElection.description: Enable leader election. Required when replicaCount > 1; harmless at 1.
leaderElection.title: leaderElection

logLevel.description:
DEPRECATED: use the top-level `logging.level` instead. Kept as a fallback
for existing values files — `logging.level` wins when both are set. Applies to
the controller and webhook (RUST_LOG style: error|warn|info|debug|trace).
logLevel.title: logLevel

probePort.description: Liveness/readiness HTTP probe port on the controller (serves /healthz, /readyz, /metrics).
probePort.title: probePort

replicaCount.title: replicaCount

resources.description:
Resource requests/limits for the controller pod.
No CPU limit (CPU throttling on an operator only adds reconcile latency; the
request reserves a fair share). The memory limit must cover the *burst* on
startup/restart, not just steady state (~120Mi): on (re)start the controller
reconciles every existing resource at once, spawning concurrent in-process
`kopia` subprocesses (whose RSS counts against this container's cgroup) to
list/connect a repository that may hold many snapshots. With the
OpenTelemetry/OTLP stack linked in, 256Mi was too tight — the burst OOMKilled
the controller, which then crash-looped (OOM -> restart -> re-reconcile burst
-> OOM). 1Gi gives ample headroom. See crates/e2e/tests/lifecycle.rs.
resources.title: resources

fullnameOverride.description: Override the full release-qualified name (defaults to "<release>-kopiur").
fullnameOverride.title: fullnameOverride

global.description: Global values are values that can be accessed from any chart or subchart by exactly the same name.
global.title: global

grafanaDashboard.description:
Grafana dashboard for the kopiur fleet. The same JSON lives in
deploy/dashboards/kopiur.json (the single source of truth, copied into the
chart by `cargo xtask gen-all`) for manual import. By default it ships as a
ConfigMap labeled for the Grafana sidecar to auto-discover; flip
grafanaOperator.enabled to render a grafana-operator GrafanaDashboard CR from
the very same JSON instead.

annotations.description: Extra annotations added to the dashboard object (ConfigMap or CR).
annotations.title: annotations

enabled.description: Create the dashboard (a sidecar ConfigMap by default).
enabled.title: enabled

folderAnnotation.description: Annotation setting the Grafana folder for the sidecar ConfigMap (optional).
folderAnnotation.title: folderAnnotation

allowCrossNamespaceImport.description: Allow a Grafana in any namespace to import this GrafanaDashboard.
allowCrossNamespaceImport.title: allowCrossNamespaceImport

enabled.description: Render a grafana-operator GrafanaDashboard CR instead of the sidecar ConfigMap.
enabled.title: enabled

folder.description: Folder to create the dashboard in (Grafana folder name).
folder.title: folder

matchLabels.description: spec.instanceSelector.matchLabels — selects which Grafana instance(s) load this dashboard.
matchLabels.title: matchLabels

resyncPeriod.description: How often grafana-operator re-checks the dashboard for updates.
resyncPeriod.title: resyncPeriod

label.description: Label the Grafana sidecar watches for (key: value). Adjust to your stack.
label.title: label

labels.description: Extra labels added to the dashboard object (ConfigMap or CR).
labels.title: labels

namespace.description: Namespace for the dashboard object; defaults to the release namespace.
namespace.title: namespace

grafanaDashboard.title: grafanaDashboard

image.description:
=============================================================================
Images
=============================================================================
Three images ship with kopiur:
  * controller — the operator (reconcilers); exposes /metrics.
  * webhook    — the axum admission server (validating + mutating).
  * mover      — the per-Job image (one Job per Backup/Restore/Maintenance run).

Per ADR §G15 the mover image MUST be reproducibly pinned: prefer `digest`
over a floating tag in production so a re-pulled `:latest` can never change
what runs in a data-protection Job. When `digest` is set it wins over `tag`.

digest.description: Pin by digest (e.g. "sha256:..."); takes precedence over tag.
digest.title: digest

mover.description:
Mover (per-Job) image. The controller reads this reference and stamps it
into every Job it creates. Digest-pinned recommended (ADR §G15).

digest.description: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED in production. Digest pin for the mover Job image.
digest.title: digest

pullPolicy.description: Pull policy used on the mover Job pods.
pullPolicy.title: pullPolicy

mover.title: mover

pullPolicy.description: Default image pull policy for controller and webhook.
pullPolicy.title: pullPolicy

registry.description: Container registry shared by all three images unless overridden per-image.
registry.title: registry

image.title: image

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets applied to controller/webhook pods and mover Jobs.
imagePullSecrets.title: imagePullSecrets

installCRDs.description:
Install the 7 kopiur.home-operations.com CRDs as part of this release.
When true the CRDs are rendered under templates/ (guarded by this flag) so
`helm uninstall` removes them and `installCRDs: false` actually omits them.
Trade-off: templated CRDs are subject to Helm's normal apply ordering and
(unlike the special `crds/` directory) are NOT skipped on `helm upgrade`.
That is the behavior we want for an alpha API. If you manage CRDs out of band
(GitOps applies deploy/crds/*.yaml directly), set this to false.
installCRDs.title: installCRDs

installScope.description:
"namespaced" (default) or "cluster".
  namespaced — RBAC is a namespace-scoped Role/RoleBinding. The operator
    manages kopiur.home-operations.com resources only in its own namespace. ClusterRepository
    is NOT reconciled (it is a cluster-scoped kind and out of a Role's reach).
  cluster    — RBAC is a ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding. The operator manages
    kopiur.home-operations.com resources cluster-wide AND reconciles ClusterRepository.
Per ADR §4.11 namespaced is the safer default; cluster scope is an explicit
opt-in for platform teams running a shared backup tier.
installScope.title: installScope

logging.description:
=============================================================================
Logging (stdout / `kubectl logs`)
=============================================================================
Controls the fmt logging that every component writes to stdout. The controller
passes RUST_LOG + KOPIUR_LOG_FORMAT through to mover Jobs, so a mover honors the
same level and format. Env var names match crates/telemetry/src/env.rs.

format.description:
Console format: "text" (human-readable, default) or "json" (one structured
object per line for Loki/ELK/Datadog). Unknown values degrade to text.
format.title: format

level.title: level

logging.title: logging

enabled.description: Expose a Service for the controller's /metrics endpoint.
enabled.title: enabled

backupStaleAfterSeconds.description: Age (seconds) after which a BackupConfig's last success is "stale".
backupStaleAfterSeconds.title: backupStaleAfterSeconds

enabled.description: Create a Prometheus-Operator PrometheusRule with kopiur alerts.
enabled.title: enabled

labels.description: Extra labels (e.g. to match your Prometheus ruleSelector).
labels.title: labels

enabled.description: Create a Prometheus-Operator ServiceMonitor. Requires the CRD to exist.
enabled.title: enabled

labels.description: Extra labels (e.g. to match your Prometheus serviceMonitorSelector).
labels.title: labels

nameOverride.description: Override the chart name used in resource names (defaults to .Chart.Name = "kopiur").
nameOverride.title: nameOverride

observability.description:
=============================================================================
OpenTelemetry (OTLP) — traces, logs, and a metrics push, off by default
=============================================================================
When enabled, the controller, webhook, and mover Jobs export OTLP to the
configured collector (the controller passes the same OTEL_* env to movers).
Metrics are ALSO always available via the Prometheus /metrics pull endpoint;
OTLP just adds a push path plus traces and logs. Env var names match
crates/telemetry/src/env.rs.

enabled.description: Enable OTLP export (sets OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT on all components).
enabled.title: enabled

endpoint.description: Collector gRPC endpoint. Required when enabled. Only gRPC is compiled in.
endpoint.title: endpoint

extraEnv.description: Extra raw env (e.g. OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER) added to every component.
extraEnv.title: extraEnv

headers.description: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS, e.g. "authorization=Bearer xyz". Empty to omit.
headers.title: headers

protocol.description: OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL (only "grpc" is supported by this build).
protocol.title: protocol

strict.description: Fail-fast on telemetry misconfiguration instead of degrading to fmt+pull.
strict.title: strict

observability.title: observability

annotations.description: Extra annotations (e.g. IRSA / Workload Identity role bindings).
annotations.title: annotations

create.description: Create the ServiceAccount. Disable to bring your own.
create.title: create

name.description: Name to use; defaults to the chart fullname when empty.
name.title: name

webhook.description:
=============================================================================
Admission webhook
=============================================================================
Implemented as a SEPARATE Deployment + Service (cleaner than folding it into
the controller) per ADR §5.3. The Service maps 443 -> the container's 8443.

caBundle.description:
Base64-encoded PEM CA bundle injected into the webhook configurations
when certManager.enabled is false. Required for the API server to trust a
self-managed serving cert. Ignored when certManager.enabled is true.
caBundle.title: caBundle

enabled.description:
Provision a cert-manager Certificate + self-signed Issuer for the
webhook, and let cert-manager's ca-injector populate caBundle on the
webhook configurations (via the cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation).
When false you must supply webhook.tls.secretName yourself and set
webhook.caBundle (base64 PEM) so the API server trusts the webhook.
enabled.title: enabled

issuerRef.description:
Use an existing Issuer/ClusterIssuer instead of the self-signed Issuer
this chart creates. Leave name empty to use the chart-managed Issuer.
issuerRef.title: issuerRef

containerPort.description: Port the webhook container listens on (must match listenAddr above).
containerPort.title: containerPort

enabled.description:
Deploy the webhook (Deployment + Service + Validating/Mutating configs).
When false, validation falls back to the controller's defensive checks only.
enabled.title: enabled

failurePolicy.description:
failurePolicy for both webhook configurations: Fail (fail-closed,
recommended for a backup operator — ADR §7.2) or Ignore.
failurePolicy.title: failurePolicy

listenAddr.description: Address the webhook server binds to (env KOPIUR_WEBHOOK_ADDR).
listenAddr.title: listenAddr

resources.description:
No CPU limit (see controller.resources). The webhook links the same
OpenTelemetry/OTLP stack but does no kopia work, so it stays light; the
generous memory limit is pure headroom.
resources.title: resources

serviceMonitor.description: The webhook serves /metrics on its TLS port (kopiur_webhook_admission_total).

enabled.description: Create a ServiceMonitor scraping the webhook's /metrics over HTTPS.
enabled.title: enabled

insecureSkipVerify.description: The webhook serves a self-signed cert, so skip verification by default.
insecureSkipVerify.title: insecureSkipVerify

serviceMonitor.title: serviceMonitor

tls.description:
TLS for the webhook server. The webhook ALWAYS serves TLS (k8s requires
HTTPS for admission). Two ways to provide the serving cert:

secretName.description:
Name of the Secret holding tls.crt / tls.key (and optionally ca.crt).
When certManager.enabled is true this is the Secret cert-manager writes.
Otherwise YOU must create this Secret before install (kubernetes.io/tls).
secretName.title: secretName

tls.title: tls

webhook.title: webhook
